head_commit.message:
Introduce FileMode for positional I/O semantics

Replace the ad-hoc `check_seekable()` and `is_offset_aware()` methods
in the `FileIo` trait with a unified `mode() -> FileMode` interface,
modeled after Linux's `struct file::f_mode` field. Each `FileIo`
implementation now declares its capabilities (LSEEK, PREAD, PWRITE,
STREAM) through `FileMode` bitflags.

The `InodeHandle` pread/pwrite path checks `FileMode::PREAD`/`PWRITE`
instead of the coarser `is_seekable()`. This distinction lets nsfs files
(which support pread/pwrite but not lseek) return EINVAL from the
underlying I/O implementation, while pipes (which support none of them)
still return ESPIPE — matching Linux behavior.

The seekability check is also moved before the access-rights check in
`read_at()`/`write_at()`, ensuring that positional I/O on pipes always
yields ESPIPE regardless of the file descriptor's read/write mode.

Signed-off-by: Zhenchen Wang <m202372036@hust.edu.cn>
Signed-off-by: Zhihang Shao <dio_ro@outlook.com>
